vulnerabilities.title: Shescape has potential environment variable exposure on Windows with CMD
Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity in shescape
Shescape on Windows escaping may be bypassed in threaded context
vulnerabilities.description:
### Impact

This may impact users that use Shescape on Windows in a threaded context (e.g. using [Worker threads](https://nodejs.org/api/worker_threads.html)). The vulnerability can result in Shescape escaping (or quoting) for the wrong shell, thus allowing attackers to bypass protections depending on the combination of expected and used shell.

This snippet demonstrates a vulnerable use of Shescape:

```javascript
// vulnerable.js

import { exec } from "node:child_process";
import { Worker, isM

vulnerabilities.title: Shescape potential environment variable exposure on Windows with CMD
Exposure of home directory through shescape on Unix with Bash
Shescape prior to 1.5.8 vulnerable to insufficient escaping of line feeds for CMD
Null characters not escaped
Shescape vulnerable to insufficient escaping of whitespace
Shescape has possible misidentification of shell due to link chains
vulnerabilities.description:
### Impact

This impacts users of Shescape that configure their `shell` to point to a file on disk that is a link to a link. The precise result of being affected depends on the actual shell used and incorrect shell identified by Shescape.

In particular, an attacker may be able to bypass escaping for the shell being used. This can result, for example, in exposure of sensitive information, consider the following proof of concept (targeting Shescape v2):

```javascript
import fs from "node:fs";
im
